title: check:all standard: frozen template scripts, per-repo config
rationale: Fleet-wide byte-identity makes the gate surface learn-once; cmp against templates/l5-toolkit is the conformance check, so any local edit (even formatting) is drift.

description: The fleet-frozen check-all.ts / check-ci-parity.ts templates do not satisfy warren's Biome formatter at lineWidth 100 (they fail even the l5-toolkit's own biome baseline), so bun run lint broke immediately after copying them in.
resolution: Added a biome.json overrides block disabling the formatter for exactly scripts/check-all.ts and scripts/check-ci-parity.ts (linter stays on). Never reformat the frozen scripts — that breaks cmp byte-identity with the template.

description: check:coverage analytics failures are environmental (system clock past hardcoded test dates), not a regression.
resolution: Make these tests pass an explicit ?from/?to window (or freeze 'now') instead of relying on the default 30-day window. Tracked separately; warren-0b75 work is unrelated and otherwise green.

description: Measuring `bun run check:coverage` in a sandbox where node_modules is MISSING produces an INFLATED coverage percentage, not a depressed one. When a test file fails to import ('Cannot find package drizzle-orm/zod/@os-eco/burrow-cli/...'), Bun's coverage reporter never imports the source under test, so that source is EXCLUDED from the denominator. A smaller denominator raises the %. A deps-missing sandbox reported lines 93.23% with ~208 import failures; the same repo deps-installed with ALL tests green (0 failures — exactly CI's state, since CI runs 'bun install' then 'check:coverage:ci') measured lines 92.21%. The patrol (warren-acd7) treated 93.23% as a lower bound and inferred CI >= 93.23%; the direction is inverted — 93.23% is an upper bound and CI is 92.21%. Setting a floor at (inflated_actual - margin) therefore breaks CI: a 92.98 lines floor failed because true CI coverage is 92.21%.
resolution: Before trusting any `bun run check:coverage` number for a ratchetwatch tightening: (1) run `bun install` first; (2) confirm the test run has ZERO failures (grep the summary for '0 fail' / no 'Cannot find package' errors) — import failures silently truncate the coverage denominator; (3) prefer reading the 'All files' row from a real CI run's coverage artifact over a sandbox measurement. Also: the _comment baseline note in scripts/coverage-budgets.json has been stale since 2026-06-12 (claims functions 88.85% while the floor is already 89.36) — keep it in sync when raising floors. Correct ratchet direction with true deps-installed actuals (functions 90.19% slack 0.83pt = FINDING; lines 92.21% slack 0.67pt = NOT a finding) is the OPPOSITE of what the inflated numbers suggested.
